probe: Count to three. max_tokens=5 against llama.cpp cache type variants, plus rejected Camelid attention rounding variants

baseline.notes: Camelid matches through generated tokens [28705, 28740] then picks dot while llama.cpp default f16/f16 cache picks comma.

llama_cpp_cache_type_findings.notes: Reference flips to the Camelid dot continuation when both K and V cache are f32.
Reference also matches Camelid when K is f16 and V is f32.
Reference takes a different first-token path, showing V-cache precision participates strongly in this near-tie.
Single-threaded llama.cpp still chooses comma, so this is not just thread nondeterminism.

rejected_camelid_variants: round final attention context rows to f16
round attention context accumulation after each value contribution to f16
round attention scores to f16 before softmax
round pre-normalized attention probabilities to f16
round normalized attention probabilities to f16
combine attention score/probability f16 rounding variants
current_localization: The remaining Count to three. blocker is now localized to llama.cpp default f16/f16 KV-cache attention numeric path rather than prompt construction, tokenizer, router top-k selection, or simple scalar f16 rounding in Camelid attention.
support_boundary: Evidence only; no broad Mixtral, frontend, long-context, production, neighboring-row, or full-support claim.
